15.reason: [Error: The stack capacity is 100 and top starts at -1, so the stack is full when top == 99 (MAX-1). Checking top==100 allows an out-of-bounds write.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: The condition should be top == MAX-1 (i.e., top == 99) to check if the stack is full, not top == 100. Also, the stack size is MAX (100), so the maximum index is 99.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: Should check top == MAX-1 (i.e., 99) for full stack, not top == 100. Also, MAX is 100, so valid indices are 0-99.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Condition should be top==MAX-1 or top==99; using top==100 will never detect a full stack (top never reaches 100 before overflow), and allows out-of-bounds access.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  Error: Full condition should check top == MAX-1 (99), not top == 100, because maximum index is 99.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: The stack capacity is 100 and top starts at -1, so the stack is full when top == 99 (MAX-1). Checking top==100 allows an out-of-bounds write.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The stack capacity is 100, so valid indices are 0 to 99. The stack is full when top is 99 (MAX-1). Checking top==100 allows an out-of-bounds write.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The condition should be top == MAX - 1 or top == 99, since the stack indices go from 0 to 99 for size 100. Checking top == 100 allows pushing beyond capacity.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [The condition should be top == MAX - 1 or top == 99, since top is zero-based and MAX is 100. Checking top == 100 allows pushing beyond the array bounds.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [If top==99, ++top becomes 100, writing to stack[100] which is out-of-bounds; also type mismatch (char vs int).]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  Error: stack is char, cannot store integer values correctly; also, this line is reached even if stack is full due to incorrect condition.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Assigning an int 'b' to a char array element truncates the value, leading to data corruption.]
